summary: Completed matching C decompilation for Fox/Falco neutral special Blaster code and special side-taunt/AppealS code by adding ftFox_SpecialN.c and ftFox_AppealS.c, wiring those objects into the build, replacing large portions of ftfox.s assembly with matched C, and updating Fox/Falco action-state table references to named callbacks. The PR also expanded ftfox.h with Fox action IDs, Blaster/AppealS prototypes, and a much more detailed ftFoxAttributes layout, plus small supporting header/struct constants and symbol-name propagation in related fighter/item assembly. There were no PR body comments or review comments in the slice, so intent beyond "complete - matching" is inferred from the diff.
what_changed: Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftFox/ftFox_SpecialN.c with 872 lines covering Fox/Falco Blaster helper functions, item hold-joint position helpers, Blaster action checks, clear/remove/create Blaster item logic, grounded/aerial SpecialN start actions, and SpecialN Anim/IASA/Phys/Coll callbacks.

Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftFox/ftFox_AppealS.c with 192 lines covering the Fox/Falco special taunt: usage/input checks, D-pad-down detection, action-state sequencing, Anim/IASA/Phys/Coll callbacks, and damage/death callback cleanup.
Removed most of the corresponding raw assembly from asm/melee/ft/chara/ftfox.s; the diff shows ftfox.s dropping roughly 1,730 lines of function bodies while leaving only a small assembly remainder.

Updated Falco's assembly action-state table to reference the newly named shared Fox/Falco callbacks, e.g. ftFox_SpecialNStart_Anim, ftFox_SpecialNLoop_IASA, ftFox_SpecialAirNEnd_Coll, and ftFox_AppealS_Anim/IASA/Phys/Coll instead of func_800E62A4-style labels.
Added the new C object files to obj_files.mk immediately after ftfox.c.o: ftFox_AppealS.c.o and ftFox_SpecialN.c.o.

Expanded ftfox.h with FTFOX_APPEALS_ATTACKID, an ftFoxAction enum beginning at AS_FTCOMMON = 340 and covering SpecialN/SpecialAirN/SpecialS action IDs, a documented ftFoxAttributes layout, and prototypes for AppealS, SpecialN, and throw-related functions.
Changed Fighter's Fox special attribute field at offset 0x222C from a raw u32 to HSD_GObj* x222C_blasterGObj, matching its use as a Blaster item GObj pointer.
Added common constants in include/common_structs.h for SFX_VOLUME_MAX, SFX_PAN_MAX, and HSD_BUTTON_DPAD_DOWN.
Renamed or propagated symbols in related assembly/item code, including calls to ftFox_ClearBlaster in asm/melee/it/code_8027CF30.s.

Included opportunistic cleanup outside Fox: Mr. Game & Watch attribute names changed from x4_GAMEWATCH_UNK/x14_GAMEWATCH_UNK2 to x4_GAMEWATCH_COLOR/x14_GAMEWATCH_OUTLINE, and Ness SpecialHi physics received a matching-oriented refactor in the diff.
smart_moves: Split the decompiled Fox code by behavior into ftFox_AppealS.c and ftFox_SpecialN.c instead of keeping a monolithic ftfox.c, making shared Fox/Falco Blaster and special-taunt logic easier to search and replace in assembly tables.
Kept Falco action-state table entries pointing to ftFox_* callback names, documenting that these Fox functions are shared by both Fox and Falco rather than duplicating Falco-specific wrappers.
Typed the Fox special-state field x222C as HSD_GObj* x222C_blasterGObj, which makes Blaster ownership and cleanup code self-documenting and avoids treating object pointers as integers.

Documented the ftFoxAttributes layout by move family, turning anonymous x0/x4/x8 fields into named Blaster, Illusion, Firefox, and Reflector attributes. This gives future Fox/Falco special-move work concrete offsets to reuse.

Preserved matching notes directly in code comments, including decomp.me scratch links and a comment that `Fighter* fp = fp = getFighter(fighter_gobj);` was needed because a double initialization affected inlined matching.
Used named constants for button and SFX magic numbers where touched: HSD_BUTTON_DPAD_DOWN, SFX_VOLUME_MAX, SFX_PAN_MAX, and FTFOX_APPEALS_ATTACKID.
decomp_lessons: For shared character code, name the function for the owner module when evidence points there, even if another character's action-state table calls it. In this PR, Falco Blaster and special-taunt entries reuse ftFox_* callbacks.

Action-state table decomp work often requires replacing raw func_ addresses in assembly tables with final C symbol names before the object fully disappears; table references are part of the matching story.
When an unknown fighter special field is consistently passed to item routines and cleared as an object handle, promote it to a typed HSD_GObj* field in Fighter rather than leaving it as u32.

Do not ignore callback fields while matching fighter moves: Blaster cleanup uses x21DC_callback_OnTakeDamage and x21E4_callback_OnDeath2, while AppealS uses x21E0_callback_OnDeath and clears it after func_801E2AF4.

Small inline helper shape can matter. The diff includes explicit inline helpers such as ftFox_SpecialN_SetNULL, ftFox_SpecialN_SetCall, ftFox_AppealS_GetLR, and comments about double-initializing `fp` to reproduce compiler output.
Header work is part of matching: adding exact action enums, attribute offsets, callback prototypes, and common constants reduces magic numbers and lets assembly tables resolve to meaningful names.
Large generated or mechanically reordered files such as include/functions.h may dominate diff byte counts; focus review/search on semantic source/header changes and build-object wiring.
naming_conventions: Fox/Falco neutral special callbacks follow `ftFox_SpecialNStart_Anim`, `ftFox_SpecialNLoop_IASA`, `ftFox_SpecialNEnd_Phys`, and aerial variants like `ftFox_SpecialAirNLoop_Coll`.
Special taunt functions use `ftFox_AppealS_*`, including `ftFox_AppealS_CheckIfUsed`, `ftFox_AppealS_CheckInput`, `ftFox_AppealS_Action`, and callback suffixes `Anim`, `IASA`, `Phys`, `Coll`.
Fox action-state enum names use `AS_FOX_SPECIALN_START`, `AS_FOX_SPECIALN_LOOP`, `AS_FOX_SPECIALN_END`, `AS_FOX_SPECIALAIRN_START`, etc.

Fox special-attribute fields use offset-prefixed descriptive names by move, e.g. `x10_FOX_BLASTER_ANGLE`, `x14_FOX_BLASTER_VEL`, `x18_FOX_BLASTER_LANDING_LAG`, `x1C_FOX_BLASTER_SHOT_ITKIND`, and `x20_FOX_BLASTER_GUN_ITKIND`.
The Blaster GObj special-state field is named `x222C_blasterGObj` under `fp->sa.fox`.
Common constants introduced or used include `FTFOX_APPEALS_ATTACKID`, `HSD_BUTTON_DPAD_DOWN`, `SFX_VOLUME_MAX`, and `SFX_PAN_MAX`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced decompiled functions in `asm/melee/ft/chara/ftfox.s` with C implementations while keeping remaining assembly and action-state data references intact.
Changed Falco's `as_table_falco` callback pointers from raw `func_800E...` symbols to final `ftFox_*` symbols to match shared callback addresses.
Used local stack Vec3 temporaries and exact float constants for Blaster hold-joint offsets, including `1.2325000762939453f`, `4.263599872589111f`, and `0.013600001111626625f`.
Used an explicit `#define PI64 3.141592653589793` and f64 `launchAngle` in Blaster shot creation to reproduce the angle calculation for facing direction.

Kept matching-specific inline and ordering tricks visible in comments, notably the double `fp = fp = getFighter(fighter_gobj)` initialization and small helper functions used to influence generated code.
Added object-file entries for the new C files before the residual ftfox.s object, allowing matched C to replace assembly while preserving build order.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or further type `ftFoxAttributes` fields introduced in pr-427.
Search for later cleanup of the matching workaround `fp = fp = getFighter(fighter_gobj)` in Fox SpecialN or AppealS code.
Check subsequent PRs for migration of `HSD_BUTTON_DPAD_DOWN`, `SFX_VOLUME_MAX`, and `SFX_PAN_MAX` out of common_structs.h into more specific headers.
Search for follow-up decompilation of the remaining assembly in `asm/melee/ft/chara/ftfox.s` after this PR.
Look for later PRs that split shared Fox/Falco code into neutral names or add Falco-specific wrappers.
